name: Hashing (Maps & Sets)
description: Use hash maps and sets for O(1) lookups, frequency counting, and duplicate detection.
keyIdea: Hash maps provide constant-time lookups. Convert nested loops into single passes by storing complements or frequencies.

name: Two Pointers
description: Use two indices to traverse sorted data from both ends, finding pairs or ranges efficiently.
keyIdea: Instead of O(n²) nested loops, use two pointers that converge based on conditions to achieve O(n).

name: Sliding Window
description: Maintain a dynamic window over contiguous elements to find optimal subarrays or substrings.
keyIdea: Expand the window to include elements and shrink it to exclude. Avoids recomputing from scratch.

name: Prefix Sum
description: Pre-compute cumulative sums to answer range queries in O(1) time.
keyIdea: prefix[j] - prefix[i] gives the sum of subarray [i..j-1]. Combine with hash map for subarray sum equals k.

name: Binary Search
description: Divide search space in half repeatedly to find targets in sorted data or answer-based problems.
keyIdea: Binary search works on monotonic conditions. Can search on answer space, not just arrays.

name: Sorting + Custom Comparators
description: Sort data to unlock greedy, two-pointer, and interval techniques with custom ordering.
keyIdea: Sorting brings structure. Custom comparators let you define what 'optimal' ordering means.

name: Stack
description: Use LIFO structure for parsing, matching, and finding next greater/smaller elements.
keyIdea: Stacks maintain 'last seen' context. Monotonic stacks efficiently solve next-greater-element problems.

name: Recursion
description: Solve problems by breaking them into smaller subproblems. Foundation for trees, DP, and backtracking.
keyIdea: Every recursive solution has a base case and recursive case. Think: 'What's the smallest version of this problem?'

name: Linked List Techniques
description: Manipulate pointers for reversal, merging, cycle detection, and finding intersections.
keyIdea: Use dummy nodes to simplify edge cases. Fast-slow pointers detect cycles and find midpoints.

name: Fast & Slow Pointers
description: Detect cycles and find midpoints with two-speed traversal through linked lists or arrays.
keyIdea: If fast meets slow, there's a cycle. Fast moves 2 steps, slow moves 1 step. Floyd's algorithm.

name: Monotonic Stack
description: Maintain a stack that's always sorted to efficiently find next greater/smaller elements.
keyIdea: When you see a smaller element, pop all larger elements from the stack — they found their answer.

name: Queue / Deque
description: FIFO structure for BFS, sliding window max/min, and ordered processing.
keyIdea: Deque supports O(1) operations at both ends. Sliding window max uses deque to maintain decreasing order.

name: Heap / Priority Queue
description: Efficiently find min/max elements. Essential for top-K, merging sorted streams, and scheduling.
keyIdea: Min-heap of size K tracks K largest elements. Two heaps (max+min) find medians in streams.

name: Merge Intervals
description: Sort intervals by start time, then merge or find overlaps using sweep line technique.
keyIdea: Sort by start time. If current interval overlaps with previous, merge them. Otherwise, add new interval.

name: Trie (Prefix Tree)
description: Tree structure for efficient word lookups, prefix matching, and autocomplete systems.
keyIdea: Each node represents a character. Path from root to node is a prefix. Words end at terminal nodes.

name: Binary Tree DFS
description: Traverse trees using preorder, inorder, or postorder for path finding and tree validation.
keyIdea: Preorder: visit root first. Inorder: sorted order for BST. Postorder: process children before parent.

name: Binary Tree BFS
description: Level-order traversal using queue for finding shortest paths and level-wise operations.
keyIdea: BFS naturally processes nodes level by level. Use it for shortest path in unweighted graphs/trees.

name: Graph DFS
description: Explore graphs depth-first for path finding, cycle detection, and connected components.
keyIdea: DFS explores as deep as possible before backtracking. Use visited set to avoid revisiting nodes.

name: Graph BFS
description: Breadth-first traversal for shortest path in unweighted graphs and level-wise exploration.
keyIdea: BFS guarantees shortest path in unweighted graphs. Process all neighbors before moving to next level.

name: Backtracking
description: Generate all combinations/permutations by choosing, exploring, then unchoosing.
keyIdea: Template: for each choice → make choice → recurse → undo choice. Prune branches that can't lead to solution.

name: Dynamic Programming (1D)
description: Optimize problems with overlapping subproblems using memoization or tabulation on linear states.
keyIdea: Define dp[i] = optimal answer for first i elements. Find transition from dp[i-1], dp[i-2], etc.

name: Dynamic Programming (2D)
description: Solve grid, string matching, and knapsack problems with two-dimensional state spaces.
keyIdea: Define dp[i][j] = answer for first i elements of A and first j of B. Fill table row by row.

name: Greedy Algorithms
description: Make locally optimal choices at each step to find global optimum. Prove correctness.
keyIdea: Greedy works when local optimum leads to global optimum. Sort by some criterion, then pick greedily.

name: Union Find
description: Dynamic connectivity data structure for tracking and merging connected components.
keyIdea: Union by rank + path compression gives near O(1) amortized operations. Great for connectivity queries.

name: Topological Sort
description: Order vertices in a DAG respecting dependencies. Detect cycles and schedule tasks.
keyIdea: Kahn's algorithm (BFS): repeatedly remove nodes with in-degree 0. DFS-based: reverse postorder.

name: Bit Manipulation
description: Use XOR, AND, OR, and bit shifts for efficient number manipulation and encoding.
keyIdea: XOR cancels duplicates: a^a=0, a^0=a. n & (n-1) clears lowest set bit. Use masks for subsets.
